articles.title: New NASCAR Boss Wants to Bring Back the Fun — and the 'Badass' Feeling
articles.summary: NASCAR has a new CEO, Steve O'Donnell, the first person outside the France family to lead the racing company. He says NASCAR is a 'badass American sport' and wants to make it fun again. The old CEO, Jim France, stepped down after a tough legal fight with race teams. O'Donnell promises to listen to fans and drivers.

articles.title: Watch Out for Ticks! Doctors Say This Could Be a Bad Year for Lyme Disease
articles.summary: Doctors are warning that this year might be really bad for tick bites and diseases like Lyme disease. The CDC says ER visits for tick bites are the highest since 2017. Ticks are tiny bugs that drink blood and can make you sick. They love warm weather, and this year there are a lot of mice for them to feed on. Experts say to wear bug spray and check for ticks after playing outside.

articles.title: Rocky Statue Gets a VIP Move: Inside the Art Museum for the First Time!
articles.summary: The famous Rocky statue, showing the boxer with his fists up, has moved inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art for the first time. It's part of a new exhibition called 'Rising Up' celebrating the 50th anniversary of the first Rocky movie. The statue used to stand outside the museum for over 20 years. Now it's inside with other artworks, including ancient artifacts.
